free_text: Cross sections and thermonuclear reaction rates for
41K(a,n)44Sc and 41K(a,p)44Ca.
5U Pelletron accelerator
Three targets of KBr, enriched to 99.35% in 41K, were
prepared by evaporation of KBr from outgassed tungsten
boats on to gold foils which had been etched in aqua
regia, rinsed in distilled water, and outgassed. Two
of gold foils were of a stopping thickness for 10 MeV
a-particles and the third readily transmitted 4 MeV.
The KBr was prepared from KCl, the chemical form in
which the enriched isotope was supplied, as described
Targets thicknesses (6.66+-0.40)x10**17,
Opening paren. in SAMPLE in sub 001 was
corrected. Data presentation in sub 002, 003, 004, 005
was changed.
The chamber consisted of stainless steel
tubular cross mounted at the center of neutron
detector. This was a 32x32x44 cm**3 paraffin block in
which were embedded six 3He-filled proportional
counters, the outputs of which were summed
125 cm**3 Ge(Li) detector 2.3 cm from the
target in the 0 degr direction
